Class GUIManager

java.lang.Object
io.github.isoyigido.basic.gui.core.GUIManager
public final class GUIManager extends Object
Manages switching GUIs, global overlays, alerts, and global key binds. Provides methods for changing the current GUI, changing the mouse cursor, moving the mouse cursor, taking a screenshot, setting global key binds, setting global overlays, and making alerts.
See Also:
  • GUI
  • Overlay
  • Alert

    Returns the current GUI.

    getCursor()
    Returns the current mouse cursor.

    Returns an Optional containing the current overlay, or an empty Optional if there is currently no overlay.

    Moves the mouse cursor to the given location on the screen.

  • Method Details

    • setPanel

      public static void setPanel(BasicPanel panel)
      Sets the panel that holds the GUI manager.
      Parameters:
      panel - the BasicPanel object
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input panel is null

    • setGUI

      public static void setGUI(GUI gui)
      Sets the given GUI as the current GUI.
      Parameters:
      gui - the new GUI that will be set
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input gui is null

    • setGUI

      public static void setGUI(Supplier<GUI> guiSupplier)
      Uses the given supplier to set the current GUI.
      Parameters:
      guiSupplier - the supplier for the new GUI that will be set
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input guiSupplier is null

    • setGUI

      public static void setGUI(String path)

      Loads and sets the GUI from the GUI file at the given path relative to the resources folder. Loads the GUI using GUILoader.load(String) and sets it using setGUI(GUI).

      Special cases:

      • Logs a warning and does nothing if no GUI file exists at the given path, or if an error occurs while loading the GUI file
      Parameters:
      path - the path to the GUI file relative to the resources folder (e.g., /gui/menu.gui)
      See Also:
      • GUILoader.load(String)

    • render

      public static void render(Graphics2D g)
      Forwards the render call to the current GUI, the global overlay, and active alerts.
      Parameters:
      g - the graphics context to render on

    • update

      public static void update()
      Forwards the update call to the current GUI, the global overlay, and active alerts.

    • getCursor

      public static Cursor getCursor()

      Returns the current mouse cursor.

      Special cases:

      • Returns Cursors.DEFAULT if the panel linked to the displayed window is null
      Returns:
      the current mouse cursor, or Cursors.DEFAULT if the panel linked to the displayed window is null

    • setCursor

      public static void setCursor(Cursor cursor)
      Sets the mouse cursor.
      Parameters:
      cursor - the new mouse cursor
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the input cursor is null

    • moveCursorTo

      public static void moveCursorTo(int x, int y)
      Moves the mouse cursor to the given location on the screen.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate on the virtual screen
      y - the y-coordinate on the virtual screen

    • takeScreenshot

      public static BufferedImage takeScreenshot()
      Renders a frame of the displayed window on an image with matching dimensions.
      Returns:
      the rendered BufferedImage object

    • getCurrentGUI

      public static GUI getCurrentGUI()
      Returns the current GUI.
      Returns:
      the current GUI

    • getOverlay

      public static Optional<Overlay> getOverlay()
      Returns an Optional containing the current overlay, or an empty Optional if there is currently no overlay.
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the current overlay, or an empty Optional if there is currently no overlay

    • setOverlay

      public static void setOverlay(Overlay overlay)
      Sets the overlay to the given overlay.
      Parameters:
      overlay - the new overlay, or null for no overlay

    • removeOverlay

      public static void removeOverlay()
      Removes the current overlay.
      API Note:
      This method is functionally the same as GUIManager.setOverlay(null). Use this method to improve readability.

    • addAlert

      public static void addAlert(Alert alert)
      Adds the given alert to the list of active alerts.
      Parameters:
      alert - the alert to add to the list
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input alert is null

    • setGlobalKeyBind

      public static void setGlobalKeyBind(char key, Runnable action)
      Sets a global key bind. Can be called multiple times with
      Parameters:
      key - the character of the bound key
      action - the action that is run when the bound key is pressed
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input action is null
